note: Agent families are data, not code. Validators check that governance uses registered families and that cross-agent review pairs two distinct families; adding an agent family is an entry here, not a validator change. Critic model policy: never downgrade — critic runs use a high-capability model of the critic family. Transport policy: rung ceilings and retries are per-family data with a recorded basis (calibration or dated default), never bare literals in scripts; recalibrate with `harness review calibrate`. Runtime enforcement policy: each family records which harness-visibility surfaces its runtime can mechanically enforce (statusline, prompt re-injection, response check) with a dated basis; a family with no surface is a declared exception in the enforcement map, never a silent gap.

transport.ceilingBasis: initial-generous-default 2026-07-02 pending `harness review calibrate` (observed: evidence-summary 22s good-run, packet-file >120s at xhigh reasoning effort)

runtimeEnforcement.basis: Codex CLI exposes no statusline or prompt/response hook surface as of 2026-07-03; the [harness] visibility obligation is prose-declared for this family (enforcement-map: visibility-tier-line).

claude-code.highCapabilityRequirement: alias or model name containing opus, fable, or mythos

runtimeEnforcement.basis: Claude Code statusLine plus UserPromptSubmit/SessionStart/Stop hooks via .claude/settings.json, wired to harness/scripts/runtime-hooks/, 2026-07-03; wiring integrity gated by validate-runtime-adapters.mjs.
